title: Account Lock After Three Consecutive Failed PIN Attempts
description: The system shall lock the user's account after three consecutive failed PIN attempts.
rationale: Prevent brute force attacks by locking accounts after multiple unsuccessful login attempts.
business_value: Enhances security and protects against unauthorized access to user accounts.

source.description: As a user I need my account to be locked after three consecutive failed PIN attempts So that brute force attacks are prevented

acceptance_criteria: The account is locked after three consecutive failed PIN attempts.
The user is notified of the lock.
preconditions: The system has a mechanism to track and store PIN attempts.
postconditions: The account remains locked until manually unlocked by an administrator or after a defined cooldown period.
functional_constraints: The system must accurately count consecutive failed PIN attempts.
The lock status must be reset when the user enters the correct PIN.
security_requirements: Prevent brute force attacks by locking accounts after multiple unsuccessful login attempts.

non_functional_requirements.performance: Low latency in detecting and applying account locks.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available during all user sessions.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should handle a reasonable number of concurrent users without performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages should clearly indicate that the account is locked.

edge_cases: What happens if a user attempts to lock their account multiple times?
How does the system handle simultaneous login attempts from different devices?
What happens if the system experiences a failure in counting consecutive failed attempts?
dependencies: A mechanism to track and store PIN attempts
An administrative interface for unlocking accounts

interfaces.name: PIN Attempt Tracking API
interfaces.description: The system will use this API to record each PIN attempt and check the count of consecutive failed attempts.

interfaces.name: User Account Database
interfaces.description: This database stores user account information, including lock status and failed attempt counts.

interfaces.name: Login Interface
interfaces.description: The UI will display error messages when a user enters an incorrect PIN and notify the user when their account is locked.

test_cases.description: User enters correct PIN after three consecutive failed attempts.
test_cases.expected_result: The account remains unlocked, and no notification of lock is shown.

test_cases.description: User enters incorrect PIN four times consecutively.
test_cases.expected_result: The account is locked, and a notification is shown to the user.

test_cases.description: Admin attempts to unlock a locked account.
test_cases.expected_result: The admin can successfully unlock the account.
